Home

BeMap JavaScript API — Developer Guide

▶️ Run the demo now: npm install then npm start → builds the library and opens the dashboard at http://localhost:8080/examples/. 🧪 Tests: npm test  ·  📦 Build only: npm run build

Just want to USE the SDK (not develop it)? See the distribution README.github.md and INSTALL.md. To publish it online: DEPLOY.md.

BeMap JS API is a JavaScript SDK for BeNomad's mapping and geolocation platform — a unified, engine-agnostic API over MapLibre GL v5, Leaflet 1.9, and OpenLayers v10, plus services for routing, geocoding, autocomplete, isochrone, charging stations, and EV routing.

Quick start

// 1. Create a context
var ctx = new bemap.Context({
    "login": "<your account>",
    "password": "<your password>",
    "secure": true,
    "host": "bemap-beta.benomad.com"
});

// 2. Create a map (OpenLayers or Leaflet)
var map = new bemap.OlMap(ctx, "map-div").defaultLayers().move(2.35, 48.85, 12);

// 3. Add a marker
var marker = new bemap.Marker(new bemap.Coordinate(2.35, 48.85), {
    icon: new bemap.Icon({
        src: "marker.svg",
        anchorX: 0.5,
        anchorY: 1,
        anchorXUnits: "fraction",
        anchorYUnits: "fraction"
    })
});
map.addMarker(marker);

Features

  • Map display with OpenLayers (bemap.OlMap) or Leaflet (bemap.LeafletMap)
  • Markers with custom icons, text labels, clustering, and drag support
  • Polylines, Polygons, Circles with custom styling
  • Popups with HTML content
  • Routing with multi-stop, alternative routes, and route sheets
  • EV routing with charging station integration
  • Isochrone analysis (reachability zones)
  • Geocoding and reverse geocoding
  • Address autocomplete
  • WMS layers with GetFeatureInfo support
  • Event system for map and geometry interactions

Installation

See INSTALL.md for setup instructions.

Project structure

src/                   Source files (54 JS modules)
src-test/              Unit tests (Jasmine/Karma, 1004 specs)
dist/                  Built distribution files
examples/              Interactive examples and documentation
lib/                   Third-party libraries (installed via bower)
major_update/          Planning documents

Build

Install dependencies, then build the distribution files:

npm install
bower install
npx grunt

The generated files are written to dist/.

Running tests

npx grunt test

Examples

One command builds the library, serves + opens the dashboard, and live-reloads the browser on every change:

npx grunt dev          # build + serve + open + LIVE-RELOAD at http://localhost:8080/examples/
# npx grunt serve      # serve only — no build, no reload (dist/ must already be built)
# npx grunt serve --port=9000

With grunt dev running: edit anything under examples/ and the page refreshes automatically; edit library source under src/ and the bundle rebuilds, then the page refreshes. npm start is an alias for npx grunt dev.

grunt alone only builds into dist/ — it does not serve, so the page won't appear or refresh. Use grunt dev (or any static server, e.g. npx http-server -p 8080, then open http://localhost:8080/examples/).

Version

2.0.0

License

See LICENSE.md
